Land Rover Velar D300 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Velar D300 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 306DT V6 diesel typically costs £6,500–£9,500 supply only; the AJ20D6 Ingenium costs £5,500–£8,200. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Velar.

mainEntity.name: Is the Velar D300 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends which engine your D300 has. The 306DT V6 (2017–2020) is a capable engine but has a known crankshaft bearing fatigue risk — 5,000-mile oil changes are essential. The AJ20D6 Ingenium (2021+) is a more modern, reliable design with EGR cooler and cooling system watchpoints. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Velar D300 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the 306DT V6: crankshaft bearing failure, turbo contamination, and injector issues. On the AJ20D6 Ingenium: EGR cooler leaks, cooling system issues, and DPF regeneration problems. The engine choice defines the risk profile — check which era your car is from.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Velar D300 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An L560 Velar D300 is a £15,000–£35,000 vehicle — a £6,500–£8,000 recon is 19–53% of the car's value. This sits at or below our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold for most examples. A well-maintained, higher-value example justifies replacement; a tired, high-mileage one may not.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Velar D300 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D300 badge covers two engines: 306DT V6 (2017–2020) and AJ20D6 Ingenium (2021+). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: How do I prevent the common failures on a D300?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the 306DT V6: 5,000-mile oil changes are essential to prevent crankshaft bearing failure. For the AJ20D6 Ingenium: monitor coolant levels for EGR cooler leaks and replace the water pump preventatively at 60,000–80,000 miles. Both engines need regular long runs for DPF health.

problems.title: Crankshaft Bearing Failure (306DT, 2017–2020)
problems.symptoms: Cold‑start knock/rumble that fades as engine warms, metallic tapping, oil pressure warning, sudden seizure.
problems.repairCost: N/A — requires bottom‑end rebuild or replacement

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear cold knock, stop driving and book inspection today

problems.recommendation: This is the defining issue of the 306DT. The crankshaft bearing has a fatigue life. The key defence is 5,000‑mile oil changes with JLR‑spec oil, not the factory 15,000‑mile schedule. If you hear a rumble or knock on cold start, have the oil pressure checked immediately. A bottom‑end rebuild (£5,500–£7,000) is cheaper than a full engine replacement, but only if caught early.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Leak (AJ20D6, 2021+)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, burning smell, engine warning light.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before overheating)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler on the AJ20D6 is a known weak point. If you notice unexplained coolant loss or white smoke, have the EGR cooler inspected. Catching it early prevents overheating damage. Later revisions (post‑2022) have improved the design, but it remains a watchpoint.

problems.title: DPF Regeneration Issues (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, stop‑start unavailable.
problems.recommendation: Both engines need regular long runs for passive DPF regeneration. If your D300 is used primarily for short journeys, plan a 30–45 minute motorway run every two weeks. If the DPF warning light comes on, book a forced regeneration promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Velar D300 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only if caught before crank is scored — act on first knock
Straightforward — catch it before it causes overheating
Routine maintenance — forced regen or clean
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Velar D300? Check These First
buyIf: * 306DT (2017–2020): documented 5,000‑mile oil change history — no cold knock
* AJ20D6 (2021+): EGR cooler replaced or inspected — no coolant loss
* Regular long runs (DPF health) — or proof of forced regenerations
* Full JLR service history with correct oil spec for the engine era
avoidIf: * 306DT: cold‑start knock or rumble — walk away
* 306DT: no record of regular oil changes — this engine is unforgiving
* AJ20D6: coolant loss with no diagnosis — EGR cooler may be failing
* Any engine: engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The D300 badge represents two very different engines. The 306DT V6 is powerful but has a known Achilles heel — crank bearing failure, preventable only with aggressive oil changes. The AJ20D6 Ingenium is more modern and reliable, with EGR cooler and cooling system as its main watchpoints. Buy on full, era‑appropriate service history — and never ignore a cold knock.

cta.label: → Compare Velar D300 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Velar D300 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start (306DT) — Listen for rumble/knock that fades as engine warms — crank bearing warning
* Coolant Level (AJ20D6) — Check for unexplained loss — EGR cooler leak indicator
* Service History — including which engine era (306DT or AJ20D6) and confirmed oil‑change intervals
* Oil Clarity (306DT) — Check dipstick; metallic glitter indicates bearing wear
* DPF Status — Check for warning lights; short journeys cause clogging
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
